title: Branchless clamp for slice bounds — xor/mul pattern replaces min() without branching
trigger_patterns: min of two values without branch
start end bounds check for bytes slice
explanation: xor(v, mul(xor(v, max), lt(max, v))) is a branch-free min(v, max). When v <= max, lt returns 0, mul returns 0, outer xor returns v unchanged. When v > max, lt returns 1, mul returns xor(v, max), outer xor cancels to max. The final mul(gt(end, start), sub(end, start)) also branchlessly returns 0 if start >= end, making the keccak256 length zero.
when_to_apply: Any bounds clamping where a JUMPI can be replaced — array index clamping, slice bounds, pagination limits
when_not_to_apply: When out-of-bounds should revert rather than clamp silently
